input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen when targeting .NET 10. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but no longer works in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (and Android 15+), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` API was deprecated. The recommended approach is to use the new `KeyboardAcceleratorsChanged` event or, more specifically, to opt into edge-to-edge rendering and handle insets properly.


Actually, I need to be more precise here. The underlying Android API `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated back in Android 11, so the shift in .NET MAUI is moving toward the newer `WindowInsets` APIs for handling keyboard adjustments. For .NET 10, the solution involves either setting `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` directly in the AndroidManifest.xml with proper inset handling, or using the updated MAUI APIs that work with the newer Android framework. to edge-to-edge on Android by default, which means `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` is deprecated. The solution involves either keeping `adjustResize` in the manifest while handling keyboard insets through padding or safe area APIs, or using platform-specific event handlers to listen for `WindowInsets` changes from the IME. The newer approach uses the `WindowInsets` APIs instead. For .NET 10, I should recommend `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` as MAUI's built-in solution, or handling insets manually with platform-specific code, or wrapping content in a `ScrollView` for automatic adjustment. For a chat screen, managing the keyboard inset and adjusting bottom padding would be the most practical approach.
